query_normalized: investigating the impact of network centrality on resting-state functional connectivity in autism spectrum disorder neuroscience
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (e) as an empirical-population canonical study and criterion (b) by measuring functional connectivity (the dependent variable) in the specific domain of Autism Spectrum Disorder during resting state, even though it uses fNIRS instead of fMRI and focuses on interhemispheric connectivity rather than network centrality metrics specifically.
